titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Brown to Matthew Nathan, 17 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Robert Brown, assistant manager at the National Marginal Company in Leith, to the Under-Secretary Matthew Nathan (1862-1939).In this letter, Brown discusses the case of Henry McCambley, who was accused of fraud, stated that he has yet to be fully compensated for his attendance of the trail. He goes on to state that he was surprised at his treatment by officials regarding the case.Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/1205/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The National Margarine Company
Limited. I Ack.
II Mr Moorhead,
Crown Solicitor,
For report
19 â 1 â 16 10, Quality Street, LEITH 17th.Jan. 1916 To The Under Secretary, The Castle, Dublin. Dear Sir, A writ was issued by John Orr Moorhead, Crown Solicitor,
Belfast and was delivered to me on 27th. November. commanding me to
appear at The Ulster Winter Assizes on 1st. Dec. to give evidence on
behalf of the King agaainst Henry McCambley on a charge of fraud â
ulent conversion. I had to leave my business on 30th. Nov. to proceed to
Belfast and was unable to return home until Sunday 5th. Dec. I sent
claim for expenses to Robert Dunlop. Commissioners Office, Belfast in
the belief that he was the proper person to deal with the claim but
it afterwards transpired that the matter was one for the Crown
Solicitor, Belfast. My claim amounts to Â£10/18/11. A cheque for the
small sum of Â£4/4/0 was sent me without any explanation as to what
the sum represented. I have requested the Crown Solicitor to state what is
the meaning of the cheque for Â£4/4/0 but can get no satisfaction and
he now refers me to you.
I shall be glad if you will investigate the matter and order pay
ment of the sum due me.
